what: Dimensional Control Tools for Steel Panels
title: Dimensional Control Tools for Steel Panels
text: 🔗 Visit dimControl web documentation dimControl package provides set tools dimensional control geometric analysis steel panels. designed used together packages rgl, misc3d, Rvcg, offering complementary functions support processing analysis 3D data. tools assist different stages dimensional inspection workflow, : Comparing estimated mesh CAD reference evaluate dimensional deviations. Calculating linear developed dimensions verify panel’s conformity expected measurements. Separating components estimated mesh individual evaluation. Computing angles analyze alignment different components panel.

what: Compute vertex normals by averaging triangle normals — addNormals2
title: Compute vertex normals by averaging triangle normals — addNormals2
text: Computes normals vertices triangular mesh averaging normals triangles.

title: Compute vertex normals by averaging triangle normals — addNormals2
Compute vertex normals by averaging triangle normals — addNormals2
text: x mesh3d object. normals.tri 4 x ntri matrix triangle normals. provided, computed internally using normals.mesh3d().

title: Compute vertex normals by averaging triangle normals — addNormals2
text: mesh3d object normals field updated, containing unit normals vertex.

title: Compute vertex normals by averaging triangle normals — addNormals2
text: function simply averages normals triangles vertex. weighting area angle applied.

title: Compute vertex normals by averaging triangle normals — addNormals2
code: # Create a triangular mesh mesh <- rgl::subdivision3d(rgl::icosahedron3d(), depth = 1)  # Compute triangle normals (for more robustness, Rvcg::vcgFaceNormals() can be used) norm_tri <- normals.mesh3d(mesh)  # Compute vertex normals by averaging triangle normals mesh <- addNormals2(mesh, norm_tri)  # Show the first 5 vertex normals mesh$normals[, 1:5] #>              [,1]          [,2]          [,3]          [,4]      [,5] #> [1,] 1.574277e-16  1.166131e-17 -7.710928e-17 -1.166131e-17 0.5257311 #> [2,] 5.257311e-01  5.257311e-01 -5.257311e-01 -5.257311e-01 0.8506508 #> [3,] 8.506508e-01 -8.506508e-01  8.506508e-01 -8.506508e-01 0.0000000 #> [4,] 1.000000e+00  1.000000e+00  1.000000e+00  1.000000e+00 1.0000000

what: Angle Between a Set of Vectors and a Reference Vector — angle
title: Angle Between a Set of Vectors and a Reference Vector — angle
text: Computes angle column matrix vectors reference vector.

title: Angle Between a Set of Vectors and a Reference Vector — angle
Angle Between a Set of Vectors and a Reference Vector — angle
text: v Numeric matrix dimension d x n. column represents vector. Numeric vector dimension d. deg Logical. TRUE, returns angles degrees; FALSE, radians.

title: Angle Between a Set of Vectors and a Reference Vector — angle
text: numeric vector containing angles vector v .

title: Angle Between a Set of Vectors and a Reference Vector — angle
text: function uses angle0() calculate angle, ensuring numerical stability limiting argument acos() interval \([-1, 1]\). matrix v can number columns, vector must dimension columns v.

title: Angle Between a Set of Vectors and a Reference Vector — angle
code: v <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0,               0, 1, 0), nrow = 3) a <- c(1, 0, 0) angle(v, a, deg = TRUE) #> [1]  0 90

title: Angle Between Two 3D Vectors — angle0

what: Directional Angles of Normalized Vectors — angled
title: Directional Angles of Normalized Vectors — angled
text: Computes directional angle column matrix normalized vectors canonical axis specified dim, optionally reversed dir.

title: Directional Angles of Normalized Vectors — angled
Directional Angles of Normalized Vectors — angled
text: v Numeric matrix dimension d x n containing normalized vectors. dim Integer specifying reference dimension (1 <= dim <= d). dir Numeric scalar equal 1 -1 indicating orientation reference axis. deg Logical. TRUE, angles returned degrees; otherwise radians.

title: Directional Angles of Normalized Vectors — angled
text: numeric vector length n containing directional angles column v axis \(\pm e_{dim}\).

title: Directional Angles of Normalized Vectors — angled
text: angle computed \(\theta = \arccos(dir * v[dim, ])\). vectors must unit vectors (Euclidean norm equal 1). internal normalization performed.

title: Directional Angles of Normalized Vectors — angled
code: v <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0,               0, 1, 0,               0, 0, 1), nrow = 3) angled(v, dim = 1, dir = 1, deg = TRUE) #> [1]  0 90 90

what: Directional Angle Computation for Normalized Vectors — angled2
title: Directional Angle Computation for Normalized Vectors — angled2
text: Computes directional angle set normalized vectors reference direction specific dimension. function allows returning result degrees radians optionally considering opposite direction.

title: Directional Angle Computation for Normalized Vectors — angled2
code: angled2(v, dim, negdir = FALSE, deg = TRUE)

title: Directional Angle Computation for Normalized Vectors — angled2
text: v numeric matrix dimension d x n. column represents normalized vector. dim Integer indicating reference dimension, 1 <= dim <= d. negdir Logical. TRUE, considers negative direction (complementary angle). deg Logical. TRUE, returns angles degrees; FALSE, returns radians.

title: Directional Angle Computation for Normalized Vectors — angled2
text: numeric vector containing computed angles input vector.

title: Directional Angle Computation for Normalized Vectors — angled2
code: # Three normalized vectors in the X, Y, and Z directions v <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0,               0, 1, 0,               0, 0, 1), nrow = 3)  # Angle with respect to the X axis angled2(v, dim = 1) #> [1]  0 90 90  # Angle with respect to the Y axis angled2(v, dim = 2) #> [1] 90  0 90  # Angle with respect to the Z axis angled2(v, dim = 3) #> [1] 90 90  0

what: Angles Between Two Sets of 3D Vectors — angles
title: Angles Between Two Sets of 3D Vectors — angles
text: Computes angles corresponding vectors two sets three-dimensional vectors. column input matrices represents 3D vector.

title: Angles Between Two Sets of 3D Vectors — angles
Angles Between Two Sets of 3D Vectors — angles
text: v Numeric vector matrix least 3 rows. column represents vector. w Numeric vector matrix least 3 rows. column represents vector. deg Logical. TRUE, returns angles degrees; FALSE, radians.

title: Angles Between Two Sets of 3D Vectors — angles
text: numeric vector containing angles pair corresponding vectors.

title: Angles Between Two Sets of 3D Vectors — angles
text: function uses internal helper .list2() convert input list 3D vectors: input vector length 3, returns list containing vector. input matrix \(3 \times n\), returns list element column matrix. Angles calculated using dot product acos() function, numerical correction ensure stability.

title: Angles Between Two Sets of 3D Vectors — angles
code: v <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0,               0, 1, 0), nrow = 3) w <- matrix(c(0, 1, 0,               1, 0, 0), nrow = 3) angles(v, w, deg = TRUE) #> [1] 90 90

what: Compute the Spatial Bounds (Bounding Box) of a 3D Mesh — bbox
title: Compute the Spatial Bounds (Bounding Box) of a 3D Mesh — bbox
text: Computes minimum maximum coordinates vertices 3D mesh, returning matrix extreme values along X, Y, Z axes.

title: Compute the Spatial Bounds (Bounding Box) of a 3D Mesh — bbox
Compute the Spatial Bounds (Bounding Box) of a 3D Mesh — bbox
text: x mesh3d object containing mesh vertices vb component.

title: Compute the Spatial Bounds (Bounding Box) of a 3D Mesh — bbox
text: matrix 3 rows 2 columns containing minimum maximum coordinate values mesh vertices: row corresponds axis (X, Y, Z). first column (min) contains minimum values. second column (max) contains maximum values.

title: Compute the Spatial Bounds (Bounding Box) of a 3D Mesh — bbox
text: Internally, function converts homogeneous coordinates x$vb Euclidean coordinates using rgl::asEuclidean2(), computes per-axis ranges using apply(..., range).

title: Compute the Spatial Bounds (Bounding Box) of a 3D Mesh — bbox
code: # Create a cubic mesh cube <- rgl::cube3d()  # Compute its bounding box bbox(cube) #>   min max #> X  -1   1 #> Y  -1   1 #> Z  -1   1

what: Generate a Triangular Mesh Using Binning and Marching Cubes — binningMarchingCubes
title: Generate a Triangular Mesh Using Binning and Marching Cubes — binningMarchingCubes
text: Reconstructs triangular surface 3D point cloud returns mesh3d object. function performs linear binning using npsp::binning(), adds external zero-valued layers Z direction, removes low-density nodes, truncates high-density nodes extracts isosurface using misc3d::contour3d().

title: Generate a Triangular Mesh Using Binning and Marching Cubes — binningMarchingCubes
code: binningMarchingCubes(   panel,   resol_nbin = c(4, 4, 2),   mesh_cad = NULL,   zero_layers_z = 1,   k_factor = NULL,   trunc_factor = 3.5,   level_factor = 0.99,   cad_coverage_tol = 0.99 )

title: Generate a Triangular Mesh Using Binning and Marching Cubes — binningMarchingCubes
text: panel numeric matrix data frame three columns containing X, Y Z coordinates point cloud. resol_nbin numeric vector length three defining approximate binning resolution X, Y Z directions. Default c(4, 4, 2). mesh_cad Optional. mesh3d object used reference select merge connected mesh components. Default NULL. zero_layers_z Integer. Number zero-valued layers added binning grid Z direction. Default 1. k_factor Numeric. Factor used compute tolerance removing low-density nodes. NULL, automatically assigned according resol_nbin. Default NULL. trunc_factor Numeric. Factor used truncate high-density node weights. Default 3.5. level_factor Numeric. Factor used define isosurface extraction level truncated weights. Default 0.99. cad_coverage_tol Numeric. Minimum proportion CAD model dimensions must covered reconstructed mesh mesh_cad provided. Default 0.99.

title: Generate a Triangular Mesh Using Binning and Marching Cubes — binningMarchingCubes
text: list containing reconstructed mesh3d object, processed binning object, triangular surfaces returned misc3d::contour3d() parameters used reconstruction process.

title: Generate a Triangular Mesh Using Binning and Marching Cubes — binningMarchingCubes
text: CAD mesh provided, disconnected mesh components sorted size merged reconstructed mesh reaches specified proportion CAD model dimensions.

title: Generate a Triangular Mesh Using Binning and Marching Cubes — binningMarchingCubes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- binningMarchingCubes(   panel = panel,   resol_nbin = c(4, 4, 2),   mesh_cad = mesh_cad,   zero_layers_z = 1 )  mesh <- result$mesh  rgl::open3d() rgl::shade3d(mesh, col = "lightblue") rgl::axes3d() } # }

what: Detect Modes in a Dataset — findModes
title: Detect Modes in a Dataset — findModes
text: Detects peaks (modes) numeric dataset x using kernel density estimation. mode, determines range along X-axis density decreases toward adjacent minima. function can also plot density detected modes.

title: Detect Modes in a Dataset — findModes
Detect Modes in a Dataset — findModes
text: x Numeric vector. data modes calculated. bw Numeric. Bandwidth density estimation. Default bw = 2. Q1 Numeric 0 1. Percentile density used filter relevant peaks. Default Q1 = 0.95. plot Logical. TRUE, generates plot density detected modes. Default TRUE.

title: Detect Modes in a Dataset — findModes
text: matrix one row per detected mode two columns (min max) representing X-axis range mode.

title: Detect Modes in a Dataset — findModes
code: set.seed(123) # Seed x <- c(   rnorm(200, mean = 2, sd = 0.3),   rnorm(5, mean = 3.5, sd = 0.1),   rnorm(200, mean = 5, sd = 0.3) )  # Compute and plot modes modes <- findModes(x, bw = 0.2, Q1 = 0.5, plot = TRUE)   # X-axis ranges for each mode modes #>             min      max #> mode1 0.7072493 3.156928 #> mode2 3.9328437 6.371437

what: Extract the Boundary of a 3D Mesh — getBoundarySegments
title: Extract the Boundary of a 3D Mesh — getBoundarySegments
text: Identifies unshared edges 3D mesh, .e., edges belonging one face. edges can returned either vertex indices mesh3d object consisting corresponding segments.

title: Extract the Boundary of a 3D Mesh — getBoundarySegments
Extract the Boundary of a 3D Mesh — getBoundarySegments
text: mesh mesh3d object representing 3D mesh. malla Logical. TRUE, returns mesh3d object containing boundary segments; FALSE, returns vertex indices forming boundary edges. Default FALSE. simplify Logical. TRUE malla = TRUE, simplifies resulting mesh using cleanMesh3d_rgl(). Default TRUE.

title: Extract the Boundary of a 3D Mesh — getBoundarySegments
text: malla = FALSE, matrix vertex indices forming boundary edges. malla = TRUE, mesh3d object representing boundary segments.

title: Extract the Boundary of a 3D Mesh — getBoundarySegments
text: function adaptation rgl::getBoundary3d(). Unlike original version, detects boundary edges finding duplicates edge list, implementation uses frequency table (data.table) count many times edge appears mesh faces. Edges appear considered boundary edges. Additionally, function allows returning either vertex indices boundary edges mesh3d mesh segments, optional additional simplification.

title: Extract the Boundary of a 3D Mesh — getBoundarySegments
text: 3D plot

what: Compute face normals of a triangular mesh — normals.mesh3d
title: Compute face normals of a triangular mesh — normals.mesh3d
text: Computes unit normals triangles mesh3d object using cross products.

title: Compute face normals of a triangular mesh — normals.mesh3d
Compute face normals of a triangular mesh — normals.mesh3d
text: x mesh3d object triangular topology. Must contain elements vb (vertices) (triangle indices).

title: Compute face normals of a triangular mesh — normals.mesh3d
text: 4 x ntri matrix containing homogeneous normals triangle. column represents unit normal vector, fourth row set 1 homogeneous coordinates.

title: Compute face normals of a triangular mesh — normals.mesh3d
text: function primarily intended teaching comparison purposes. general use, recommended use Rvcg::vcgFaceNormals(), efficient robust. triangle, normal computed cross product two edges normalized unit length. vertices homogeneous coordinates (fourth row equal 1), normalized computing normals.

title: Compute face normals of a triangular mesh — normals.mesh3d
Interactive 3D View Panning — pan3d
text: Enables camera panning rgl 3D scene, allowing user move view freely within graphics window dragging mouse specified button.

title: Interactive 3D View Panning — pan3d
Interactive 3D View Panning — pan3d
text: button Integer indicating mouse button used panning: 1: left button 2: right button 3: middle button (wheel) dev RGL device ID (default rgl::cur3d()). subscene Subscene movement applied (default active subscene rgl::currentSubscene3d()).

title: Interactive 3D View Panning — pan3d
text: value returned; function sets mouse callbacks pan 3D view interactively.

title: Interactive 3D View Panning — pan3d
text: default, rgl allows rotation zoom 3D objects, camera translation. function extends interaction enabling user translate entire scene direction without altering model orientation. Internally, function uses mouse callbacks via rgl::rgl.setMouseCallbacks(), mouse button pressed dragged, projection matrix (userProjection) dynamically updated simulate smooth movement within scene. specified button pressed, function records initial cursor position (begin event). mouse movement (update event), calculates relative displacement applies translation transformation userProjection using rgl::translationMatrix(), allowing camera move drag direction enabling free movement scene graphics window.

title: Interactive 3D View Panning — pan3d
text: original code developed Duncan Murdoch part rgl package, exported. included without modifications facilitate use within package.

title: Interactive 3D View Panning — pan3d
text: Duncan Murdoch

title: Interactive 3D View Panning — pan3d
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rgl::open3d() mesh <- rgl::icosahedron3d() rgl::shade3d(mesh, col = "lightblue") pan3d(2) # Activate panning with the right mouse button } # }

title: Simulated point cloud of a steel panel — panelPoints
text: Points sampled mesh triangles probability proportional area, truncated Gaussian noise added simulate uncertainty inherent 3D scanning. dataset intended testing, comparative evaluation, demonstration dimensional control algorithms. point cloud generated sampling fixed number points triangles theoretical CAD mesh representing surface steel panel. Points assigned triangle probability proportional area. Truncated Gaussian noise zero mean bounded support added independently coordinate represent scanning uncertainty.

title: Sampling points over a triangular mesh — sampleMesh
Sampling points over a triangular mesh — sampleMesh
text: mesh list representing triangular mesh, containing: vb: 3xN (4xN homogeneous) matrix mesh vertex coordinates. : 3xM matrix vertex indices triangle (column triangle). n Integer greater 0. Total number points generate. shuffle Logical. Whether shuffle generated points randomly.

title: Sampling points over a triangular mesh — sampleMesh

title: Sampling points over a triangular mesh — sampleMesh

title: Sampling points over a triangular mesh — sampleMesh
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a triangular sphere mesh <- Rvcg::vcgSphere(1, subdiv = 1) rgl::wire3d(mesh) # visualize the mesh # Sample 10000 points over the mesh pts <- sampleMesh(mesh, 10000, shuffle = TRUE) # Plot the sampled points rgl::points3d(t(pts), col = "red", size = 5) } # }

what: Determine the Orientation of a Reinforcement Bulb — sideBulb
title: Determine the Orientation of a Reinforcement Bulb — sideBulb
text: Identifies whether bulb (upper extension reinforcement) oriented left right flat face. criterion based vertex coordinates 3D object (mesh3d): reinforcement divided according mean Z coordinate, X positions halves compared.

title: Determine the Orientation of a Reinforcement Bulb — sideBulb
Determine the Orientation of a Reinforcement Bulb — sideBulb
text: ref mesh3d object representing reinforcement. must contain vb matrix, rows correspond X, Y, Z coordinates vertices.

title: Determine the Orientation of a Reinforcement Bulb — sideBulb
text: Logical indicating bulb orientation: TRUE bulb protrudes left. FALSE bulb protrudes right.

title: Determine the Orientation of a Reinforcement Bulb — sideBulb
text: function calculates mean Z coordinate split reinforcement two parts: upper part (including bulb) lower part (excluding bulb). , compares minimum X coordinates parts determine orientation. bulb smaller X position lower part, considered oriented left.

title: Reorder Connected Edge Segments — sortSegments2

Reorder Connected Edge Segments — sortSegments2
text: edges Numeric matrix 2 rows N columns, column represents boundary segment two endpoints. first row contains first vertex segment, second row second vertex.

title: Reorder Connected Edge Segments — sortSegments2
text: numeric matrix 2 rows N columns containing segments reordered segment connected next. disconnected segments, sequence stops first gap.

what: Split a Mesh into Connected Triangle Groups — splitTrianglesInd
title: Split a Mesh into Connected Triangle Groups — splitTrianglesInd
text: Groups triangles 3D mesh independent subsets according connectivity, .e., share edge (two vertices). connected component returned group indices corresponding triangles belonging piece mesh fragment.

title: Split a Mesh into Connected Triangle Groups — splitTrianglesInd
Split a Mesh into Connected Triangle Groups — splitTrianglesInd
text: mesh mesh3d object containing mesh analyze. must include matrix, column represents triangle defined indices vertices.

title: Split a Mesh into Connected Triangle Groups — splitTrianglesInd
text: list element contains indices triangles forming connected component mesh. connections triangles, list element corresponds single triangle.

title: Split a Mesh into Connected Triangle Groups — splitTrianglesInd
text: procedure useful mesh contains multiple disconnected parts (e.g., reinforcements, panels, separate fragments) need handled separately geometric analysis, visualization, data cleaning. algorithm follows steps: Checks mesh contains matrix triangles. vertex, records triangles appears. Determines pairs triangles share edge (two common vertices). Builds undirected graph triangles nodes shared-edge connections edges. Identifies connected components graph using igraph::components().

title: Split a Mesh into Connected Triangle Groups — splitTrianglesInd
code: require(igraph) #> Loading required package: igraph #>  #> Attaching package: 'igraph' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     decompose, spectrum #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     union  # Create a mesh3d object with two disconnected triangles vb <- t(rbind(   c(0, 0, 0),   c(1, 0, 0),   c(0, 1, 0),   c(2, 0, 0),   c(3, 0, 0),   c(2, 1, 0) )) it <- t(rbind(   c(1, 2, 3),   c(4, 5, 6) )) mesh <- rgl::tmesh3d(vertices = vb, indices = it)  # Split the mesh into connected triangle groups groups <- splitTrianglesInd(mesh) str(groups) #> List of 2 #>  $ : int 1 #>  $ : int 2

what: Calculate Distance over a 3D Surface — surfaceDistance
title: Calculate Distance over a 3D Surface — surfaceDistance
text: Computes total length 3D path summing Euclidean distances consecutive points. Useful estimating distance traveled 3D surface.

title: Calculate Distance over a 3D Surface — surfaceDistance
Calculate Distance over a 3D Surface — surfaceDistance
text: segmento matrix data frame three columns (x, y, z) representing coordinates consecutive points 3D space.

title: Calculate Distance over a 3D Surface — surfaceDistance
text: numeric value indicating total distance traveled along surface.

title: 3D Cross Product — xprod
text: Computes cross product two three-dimensional vectors v w \(\mathbb{R}^3\).

title: 3D Cross Product — xprod
3D Cross Product — xprod
text: v Numeric vector length 3. w Numeric vector length 3.

title: 3D Cross Product — xprod
text: Numeric vector length 3 perpendicular v w, whose magnitude equals area parallelogram defined v w. direction vector follows right-hand rule.

title: 3D Cross Product — xprod
text: cross product defined : $$ v \times w = \begin{pmatrix} v_2 w_3 - v_3 w_2 \\ v_3 w_1 - v_1 w_3 \\ v_1 w_2 - v_2 w_1 \end{pmatrix} $$
